title: DNA Quantification -- CHEM 584
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"dmguo","text":"Protocol adapted from the NanoDrop Spectrophotometer User’s Manual. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Start a new experiment using the Nanodrop touchscreen.
Click on the corresponding application module (e.g., dsDNA).
Open the sampling arm and load a blank sample (e.g., TE Buffer, MQ Water, etc.). 2 µL
Close the sampling arm on the machine to cover the blank sample.
Click "OK" to read the blank if the Nanodrop is not already in automatic mode.
Open the sampling arm and clean the blank off the upper and lower pedestals using a Kim Wipe.
Load your DNA sample and close the sampling arm. 2 µL
Click "Measure sample" if the Nanodrop is not in automatic mode.
Take a picture of the screen that shows your results and the corresponding spectra for your records.
Clean the nucleic acid sample off of the upper and lower pedestals using a Kim Wipe.
creator: Ken Christensen
